ranked_movies.title: Pride and Prejudice
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed across multiple threads, with high praise for both the 1995 BBC miniseries and the 2005 film adaptation. It is frequently cited as a top romance and a benchmark for the genre.

ranked_movies.title: La La Land
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a personal favorite and a top-tier romance film, praised for its musical elements and bittersweet ending.

ranked_movies.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in the main AskMen thread for its stellar cast and fun, multi-dimensional plot. It is a frequently cited modern rom-com favorite.

ranked_movies.title: The Notebook
ranked_movies.reason: A polarizing film that is frequently mentioned as both a classic and the 'most overrated' romance movie. Its divisive nature generates significant discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Love Actually
ranked_movies.reason: Another divisive film that is listed as both a favorite and a worst/overrated movie. Its ensemble cast and holiday theme make it a perennial topic of debate.

ranked_movies.title: Stardust
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a favorite romance film, praised for its fantasy elements and charming transformation storyline.

ranked_movies.title: Before Sunrise
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a top-tier, unique romance film that stands out for its realistic dialogue and deep character connection. The entire trilogy is held in high regard.

ranked_movies.title: While You Were Sleeping
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a charming and adorable favorite, with Sandra Bullock's performance and the fun family dynamic being highlighted.

ranked_movies.title: Casablanca
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as the 'best' romance movie in a top comment, cementing its status as a timeless classic in the genre.

ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a contender for favorite romance movie, praised for its heartfelt story and time-travel elements.

ranked_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a top contender for favorite romance, celebrated for its unique, non-traditional take on love and relationships.

ranked_movies.title: Notting Hill
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a classic rom-com and a frequent favorite, known for its charming leads and iconic lines.

ranked_movies.title: It Happened One Night
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as an original and still-fresh romcom from the 1930s that set the standard for the genre.

ranked_movies.title: Definitely, Maybe
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a favorite romance movie, appreciated for its storytelling structure and charm.

ranked_movies.title: The Sound of Music
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in a weekly romance thread, where it was rated 9/10 and described as a captivating masterpiece and a favorite musical.

ranked_movies.title: The Gorge
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated request thread for books with similar themes. The movie itself is praised for its couple of assassins/mercenaries who fall in love.

ranked_movies.title: The Way We Were
ranked_movies.reason: Highly rated (9.5/10) in a weekly romance thread, praised for its incredible chemistry between Redford and Streisand and its writing.

ranked_movies.title: 27 Dresses
ranked_movies.reason: Rated highly (8.5/10) in a weekly romance thread and mentioned as a film that fits a specific dynamic where the female lead falls first.

ranked_movies.title: The Quiet Man
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a favorite romance film for its locale and humor, showing its enduring appeal.

ranked_movies.title: Titanic
ranked_movies.reason: A frequently discussed film that is named as both the 'worst romantic movie ever made' and a cultural touchstone, ensuring its place in the conversation.
